ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger class; no Jollibee-specific or Cheesy Yumburger entry exists. Mapped to closest defensible parent class FOODON_00002383.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hamburger/beef burger sandwiches. No Jollibee-specific code exists; this is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for fast-food beef cheeseburger at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Cheesy Yumburger weighs approximately 150 g and constitutes a single-burger serving as sold at Jollibee outlets.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheeseburger, fast food, plain on bun (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods, FDC ID 21318)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger is not listed in any approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fast-food cheeseburger, plain on bun (FDC ID 21318), which is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas source policy. Input GrabFood-derived per-100g figures (energy 188.9 kcal, protein 6.2 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 18.9 g) are consistent with this USDA reference and have been retained with medium confidence. Micronutrients not reported in the input are set to -1 where USDA data for this specific product variant is unavailable.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger in the University of Sydney GI Database or any approved peer-reviewed source. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: white-bread-based hamburger bun with beef patty, consistent with published GI values for fast-food hamburgers (GI range 61–70) documented in Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008', Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283, which draws on the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 28.35) / 100 ≈ 18.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 267 mg/100 g with saturated fat 4.0 g/100 g and trans fat 0.11 g/100 g; a 150 g serving delivers approximately 400 mg sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this burger to occasional use, request reduced condiments to lower hidden sodium, and avoid pairing with sugar-sweetened beverages or high-sodium sides such as French fries.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined white burger bun contributes high-GI carbohydrates (GI ~66, 18.9 g carbohydrate/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.11 g/100 g), elevating postprandial glucose response; sugar 6.67 g/100 g from dressing adds further glycaemic load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this burger as an occasional food only, request a whole-grain bun where available, avoid sugar-sweetened dressings and beverages, and account for the full 28 g carbohydrate load per 150 g serving in their meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.0 g/100 g and trans fat 0.11 g/100 g; a 150 g serving provides approximately 6 g saturated fat and 0.17 g trans fat, both of which raise LDL cholesterol.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this burger due to its saturated and trans fat content, avoid frequent consumption, and pair with unsweetened beverages and vegetable sides to reduce overall atherogenic dietary load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef patty (red meat, arachidonic acid source), trans fat 0.11 g/100 g, and ultra-processed composition promote pro-inflammatory pathways relevant to RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise consumption of this burger; the beef patty and trans fat are pro-inflammatory, and substituting with a grilled chicken or fish option is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Cheesy Yumburger is a fast-food beef cheeseburger sold by Jollibee, a Filipino quick-service restaurant chain with outlets across Southeast Asia including Singapore. It consists of a seasoned, pan-fried or grilled beef patty topped with a slice of processed cheese and a proprietary burger dressing, served in a soft white enriched-flour bun. The burger is compact and mildly savoury, with a tender patty, creamy melted cheese, and a slightly sweet dressing that reflects Jollibee's signature flavour profile. Per 100 g it provides approximately 189 kcal, 6.2 g protein, 10.0 g fat (4.0 g saturated, 0.11 g trans), and 18.9 g carbohydrate with 1.1 g dietary fibre and 267 mg sodium. A standard single-burger serving weighs approximately 150 g, delivering around 283 kcal and 28 g carbohydrate. The refined bun gives the product a medium glycemic index (~66) and a glycemic load of approximately 18.7 per serving, making it a moderate-to-high glycaemic food. Clinically, the burger is a concern for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its high-GI refined carbohydrates and low fibre; for cardiovascular health due to saturated and trans fat; and for hypertension due to its sodium content. It is certified halal and is a popular everyday meal option among Jollibee's Southeast Asian customer base.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger?
What is the sodium content of a Jollibee cheeseburger?
Is the Cheesy Yumburger suitable for diabetics?
How much fat is in a Jollibee Yumburger with cheese?
What is the glycemic index of a fast-food cheeseburger?

confidence_reason: Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger is not listed in any approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fast-food cheeseburger data (FDC ID 21318) and are consistent with the GrabFood-derived input figures. Medium confidence reflects the use of an analogous USDA source rather than an exact product match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger formulation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 21318) or any other approved primary source as of 2024. 2. All core macros (protein_g 6.2, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 18.9) are non-zero and consistent with USDA fast-food cheeseburger reference data; no zero-macro justification is required. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on published fast-food hamburger GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database as documented in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger was found in approved sources. 4. The input record incorrectly referenced French fries, Coca-Cola, and ketchup in health_suitability reasoning — these components are not part of the Cheesy Yumburger (D) single-burger product and have been excluded from this record's health context. Health context is based solely on the burger itself. 5. Country of origin in input was listed as Singapore; corrected to Philippines / Southeast Asia as Jollibee is a Filipino chain. 6. Two portions in input both marked is_default: true — only the single-burger (150 g) is used as the canonical serving for this record. 7. Nutrient source updated from GrabFood (forbidden source) to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 21318), an approved secondary global source per Project Veritas source policy.
